message:
fix(grep): ensure deterministic pane output ordering

Sort matching panes alphabetically before output to ensure consistent,
reproducible results across invocations:

- Add sort.Strings() call for JSON output pane list
- Collect panes into slice and sort before text output iteration

This prevents flaky test assertions and improves debuggability by
ensuring the same set of matching panes always appears in the same
order regardless of map iteration order.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1dmet","title":"Task: Implement output capture for ensemble agents in internal/ensemble/capture.go","description":"# Task: Implement Output Capture for Ensemble Agents

## Location
internal/ensemble/capture.go

## Purpose
Capture pane output from each agent in the ensemble and extract the **YAML output schema** for synthesis. Must handle large outputs and malformed YAML gracefully.

## Implementation
```go
package ensemble

type CapturedOutput struct {
    ModeID        string
    PaneName      string
    RawOutput     string
    Parsed        *ModeOutput
    ParseErrors   []error
    CapturedAt    time.Time
    LineCount     int
    TokenEstimate int
}

type OutputCapture struct {
    tmuxClient *tmux.Client
    maxLines   int  // Default 1000
    validator  *SchemaValidator
}

func (c *OutputCapture) CaptureAll(session *EnsembleSession) ([]CapturedOutput, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) capturePane(pane string) (string, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) extractYAML(raw string) (string, bool)
```

## YAML Extraction Strategy
- Prefer first fenced ```yaml block
- Fallback: detect `thesis:` root line and capture until EOF
- If multiple YAML blocks, choose the largest valid parse

## Integration with Synthesis
- Parsed ModeOutput is passed to OutputCollector
- RawOutput retained for debugging
- Token estimates via internal/tokens package
- **Do not fail if mode_id missing**; OutputCollector will inject from assignment metadata via Normalize

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Captures output from all ensemble panes
- [ ] Extracts YAML schema blocks reliably
- [ ] Handles missing/malformed output gracefully
- [ ] Token estimation for synthesis planning
- [ ] Configurable max lines to capture
- [ ] Logs per-pane capture + parse outcomes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:44.994035314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170149202Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170130136Z","close_reason":"Implemented output capture and extraction logic","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dmet","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586537664Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586517856Z","close_reason":"Core Budget & Resource Control implemented: BudgetTracker (budget.go) with per-agent/total tracking and enforcement, EarlyStopDetector (early_stop.go) with findings rate and similarity thresholds, token estimation via internal/tokens, ContextPackCache (context_cache.go) with TTL and invalidation. All components have comprehensive unit tests and integration tests. EarlyStop is wired into pipeline stage2. TUI dashboard (metrics_panel.go) and detailed metrics are scoped to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- ntm ensemble list

 {"id":"bd-1ed7","title":"E2E Tests: Full session recovery workflow","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for Session Recovery

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Clean session save/restore**
   - Spawn session with 3 agents
   - Execute some commands
   - Kill session (simulate crash)
   - Run ntm recover
   - Verify all panes restored with scrollback

2. **Partial recovery**
   - Spawn session
   - Corrupt one pane's state file
   - Recover
   - Verify remaining panes restored
   - Verify error reported for corrupted pane

3. **Recovery after long idle**
   - Spawn session
   - Wait 30+ seconds
   - Kill and recover
   - Verify state still valid

4. **Multi-project recovery**
   - Spawn sessions for 2 projects
   - Kill both
   - Recover each independently

### Detailed Logging
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Spawning session: %d panes", numPanes)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] State captured: %d bytes", stateSize)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Simulating crash...")
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Restoring pane %d/%d: %s", i, total, paneName)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Scrollback match: %v (%d lines)", match, lines)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:50.783368496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380086093Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380006103Z","close_reason":"E2E test suite implemented with 5 comprehensive tests: TestFullSessionRecovery (full spawn/crash/recover cycle), TestPartialRecovery (corruption handling), TestRecoveryAfterLongIdle (idle period resilience), TestMultiProjectRecovery (cross-project recovery), TestRecoveryContextBuilding (context validation). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-3law","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1m6sw","title":"DCG: Implement dcg binary detection and availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect if dcg binary is available on PATH.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'dcg' binary exists on PATH using exec.LookPath
2. Verify dcg version is compatible (parse 'dcg --version' output)
3. Cache result to avoid repeated lookups
4. Graceful degradation if dcg not found (log warning, continue without)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/dcg/detect.go

```go
package dcg

import (
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
)

var (
    once     sync.Once
    available bool
    version  string
)

func IsAvailable() bool {
    once.Do(detect)
    return available
}

func detect() {
    path, err := exec.LookPath("dcg")
    if err != nil {
        return
    }
    // Run 'dcg --version' and parse
    // Set available = true if compatible
}
```

## Testing
- Test with dcg installed
- Test without dcg installed
- Test with incompatible dcg version","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:44:08.896719039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:48:38.239365538Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:48:38.239315283Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m6sw","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mcvs","title":"Task: Add rate_limit_detected field with pattern matching","description":"## What

Add `rate_limit_detected` boolean field that pattern-matches pane output
for rate limit indicators.

## Why

Rate limiting is the #1 failure mode in multi-agent sessions. Operators
currently must manually grep pane output to detect this. Automated detection
enables automated response (wait, switch account, skip pane).

## Rate Limit Patterns

Known patterns to detect:

| Provider | Pattern |
|----------|---------|
| Claude/Anthropic | "You've hit your limit" |
| Claude/Anthropic | "resets .* (America/.*)" |
| OpenAI | "Rate limit exceeded" |
| OpenAI | "Too many requests" |
| Google | "RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED" |
| Generic | "rate limit" (case insensitive) |

## Implementation

```go
var rateLimitPatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)you've hit your limit`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)rate limit`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)too many requests`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`resets \d+[ap]m`),
}

func detectRateLimit(paneContent string) (bool, string) {
    // Check last 50 lines only (performance)
    lines := strings.Split(paneContent, "\n")
    if len(lines) > 50 {
        lines = lines[len(lines)-50:]
    }
    content := strings.Join(lines, "\n")
    
    for _, pattern := range rateLimitPatterns {
        if match := pattern.FindString(content); match != "" {
            return true, match
        }
    }
    return false, ""
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 4,
    "rate_limit_detected": true,
    "rate_limit_match": "You've hit your limit",
    "rate_limit_reset": "4pm ET"  // Parsed if available
  }]
}
```

## Performance

- Only check last 50 lines (configurable)
- Compile patterns once at startup
- Short-circuit on first match

## Configuration

Allow custom patterns via config for unknown providers:
```yaml
rate_limit_patterns:
  - "custom rate limit message"
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Each pattern with mock content
- Integration test: Actual rate-limited pane","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:18:44.314512352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:56:06.806832590Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:56:06.806782305Z","close_reason":"Task already implemented. Rate limit detection is fully functional:

1. rateLimitPatterns defined in internal/robot/status_enrich.go:28-34 with:
   - (?i)you've hit your limit
   - (?i)rate limit
   - (?i)too many requests
   - RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
   - resets \d+[ap]m

2. detectRateLimit() function at lines 154-161
3. enrichAgentStatus() integrates detection at lines 74-77
4. Agent struct has RateLimitDetected and RateLimitMatch fields
5. All rate limit tests pass (TestBackoffManager_RecordRateLimit, TestParseRateLimitWait, etc.)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mcvs","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1mdym","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-modes and --robot-ensemble-presets","description":"# Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-modes and --robot-ensemble-presets

Implement robot commands for listing available modes and ensembles.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-modes [--category CATEGORY] [--tier core|advanced|experimental|all] [--limit N] [--offset N]
ntm --robot-ensemble-presets
```

- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per robot-api-design.md (`--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`).
- `--category` accepts code (A-L) or name (Formal, Ampliative, ...).

## Output Schema

### Full Mode List (default = core tier only)
```json
{
  "action": "ensemble_modes",
  "modes": [
    {
      "id": "deductive",
      "code": "A1",
      "tier": "core",
      "name": "Deductive Inference",
      "category": {"code": "A", "name": "Formal"},
      "short_desc": "Classical logic reasoning",
      "description": "...",
      "outputs": "Valid/invalid argument classification",
      "best_for": ["Contract validation"],
      "differentiator": "Unlike induction (B1)...",
      "failure_modes": ["Hidden premises"],
      "icon": "⊢",
      "color": "#A1A1A1"
    }
  ],
  "categories": [
    {"code": "A", "name": "Formal", "mode_count": 8}
  ],
  "default_tier": "core",
  "total_modes": 80,
  "core_modes": 28,
  "advanced_modes": 52,
  "experimental_modes": 0
}
```

### Category + Tier Filter
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-modes --category Formal --tier advanced
```

### Presets List
```json
{
  "action": "ensemble_presets",
  "presets": [
    {
      "name": "project-diagnosis",
      "display": "Project Diagnosis",
      "description": "Comprehensive project health analysis",
      "modes": ["systems-thinking", "failure-mode", "perspective-taking"],
      "synthesis": {"strategy": "adversarial"},
      "budget": {"total": 30000},
      "allow_advanced": false
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation
```go
// internal/robot/ensemble_modes.go
func HandleEnsembleModes(category, tier string, limit, offset int) (*EnsembleModesResponse, error)
func filterByCategory(modes []Mode, category string) []Mode
func filterByTier(modes []Mode, tier string) []Mode
func buildCategorySummary(modes []Mode) []CategoryInfo
```

### File Location
internal/robot/ensemble_modes.go
internal/robot/ensemble_presets.go

### Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog (bd-qn9gc)
- Tier metadata support (bd-3806s)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default output only includes core tier
- [ ] Tier filter works (core/advanced/experimental/all)
- [ ] Category filter accepts code or name
- [ ] JSON includes tier + counts (incl. experimental)
- [ ] RobotResponse envelope compliance
- [ ] Arrays initialized to [] when empty","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:59.724643849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T20:14:10.493525530Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-1ovi8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-264n","title":"Add bead dependency awareness - don't assign blocked beads, reassign when blockers complete","description":"## Overview
Make ntm assign aware of bead dependencies to avoid assigning blocked beads and enable automatic reassignment when blockers complete.

## Problem
Without dependency awareness:
- Agent gets assigned a blocked bead, wastes time
- When blocker completes, no automatic reassignment happens
- Manual intervention required

## Solution
Integrate with BV's dependency graph to:
1. Filter out blocked beads during assignment
2. Watch for blocker completion
3. Auto-queue newly unblocked beads

## Implementation
```go
type DependencyAwareAssigner struct {
    BVClient        *BVClient
    AssignmentStore *AssignmentStore
    Detector        *CompletionDetector
}

func (a *DependencyAwareAssigner) GetAssignableBeads(limit int) []Recommendation {
    // Only return beads where IsActionable == true (no blockers)
}

func (a *DependencyAwareAssigner) OnCompletion(event CompletionEvent) []Recommendation {
    // Check what beads are now unblocked
    // Return newly assignable beads
}

func (a *DependencyAwareAssigner) WatchAndReassign(ctx context.Context) {
    // Background goroutine that:
    // 1. Listens for completion events
    // 2. Checks for newly unblocked beads
    // 3. Assigns to idle agents
}
```

## Assignment Flow
1. Query bv for recommendations
2. Filter to actionable only (blocked_by empty)
3. Assign to idle agents
4. On completion event:
   a. Mark bead complete in assignment store
   b. Query bv for newly unblocked beads
   c. If idle agents exist, assign new beads
   d. If no idle agents, queue for next available

## Error Handling
```go
type DependencyError struct {
    Phase  string  // "fetch", "filter", "unblock_check", "reassign"
    Cause  error
    Beads  []string  // Affected bead IDs
}

// Error scenarios and handling:
//
// 1. BV unavailable
//    - Fall back to br list --status open (no graph awareness)
//    - Log: "[DEP] BV unavailable, using br list fallback"
//    - Set warning flag in assignment result
//
// 2. BV returns cycles
//    - Exclude all beads in cycle from assignment
//    - Log: "[DEP] Cycle detected: %v - excluding from assignment"
//    - Return warning in assignment result
//    - User must fix cycles manually
//
// 3. Blocker completion race condition
//    - Multiple completions trigger same unblock check
//    - Use mutex around unblock detection
//    - Dedup newly unblocked beads
//
// 4. Stale dependency graph
//    - BV cache may be stale (30s default)
//    - On unblock check, force refresh: bv --robot-triage --no-cache
//    - Log: "[DEP] Refreshing dependency graph"
//
// 5. Orphan bead (references non-existent blocker)
//    - Treat as actionable (blocker may have been deleted)
//    - Log: "[DEP] bd-%s blocked by unknown bd-%s, treating as actionable"
//
// 6. Reassignment failure
//    - Keep bead in unblocked queue
//    - Retry on next idle agent
//    - Log: "[DEP] Reassign failed for bd-%s, queuing"
```

## Cycle Handling
```go
func (a *DependencyAwareAssigner) HandleCycles(insights *Insights) {
    if len(insights.Cycles) == 0 {
        return
    }
    
    for _, cycle := range insights.Cycles {
        cycleSet := make(map[string]bool)
        for _, id := range cycle {
            cycleSet[id] = true
        }
        a.ExcludedBeads = append(a.ExcludedBeads, cycleSet)
    }
    
    log.Printf("[DEP] Excluding %d beads in cycles from assignment", 
        countExcluded(a.ExcludedBeads))
}
```

## Notification on Unblock
When a bead becomes unblocked:
- Log: "[UNBLOCK] bd-xyz now ready (was blocked by bd-abc)"
- If --auto-reassign: Assign to next idle agent
- If manual: Show in status output

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ntm assign only assigns actionable beads
- [ ] Completion triggers unblock check
- [ ] Newly unblocked beads detected correctly
- [ ] Auto-reassign to idle agents (configurable)
- [ ] Queue beads when no idle agents
- [ ] Clear logging of dependency decisions
- [ ] Handles cycles (excludes, warns)
- [ ] Falls back gracefully when BV unavailable
- [ ] Handles orphan blockers
- [ ] Handles stale dependency graph

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Filter blocked beads correctly
- Unit test: Detect unblock after completion
- Unit test: Queue management
- Unit test: Cycle exclusion
- Unit test: BV unavailable fallback
- Unit test: Orphan blocker handling
- Unit test: Race condition prevention
- E2E test: Multi-bead dependency chain completion","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-26c53","title":"EPIC: Flywheel Tool Bridges (Robot-first)","description":"## Goal
Integrate missing Flywheel tools into NTM with consistent **robot-first** interfaces, tool health reporting, and graceful degradation.

## Tools In Scope (missing or partial)
- DCG (Destructive Command Guard)
- SLB (two-person approval)
- RU (Repo Updater)
- MS (Meta Skill)
- UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner) – adapter + robot wrapper
- ACFS (Flywheel setup/bootstrapping)
- GIIL (image fetch for remote debugging)
- XF (X archive search)
- **JFP** (JeffreysPrompts CLI) – prompt library + skill install

## Principles
- **Robot-first**: every integration has stable JSON output
- **Graceful degradation**: tool missing -> clear hint, no crash
- **Single registry**: use internal/tools adapters for detection/version/health

## Non-Goals
- Rebuild tool functionality inside NTM
- Add new orchestration engines

## Success Criteria
- `ntm --robot-tools` or equivalent returns accurate inventory
- Each tool has a lightweight wrapper command in NTM
- Missing tools surface actionable install hints

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-26ep7","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Diagnose","description":"E2E tests for Robot Diagnose with actual sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: All Healthy
Spawn 3 agents, wait for init, verify overall_health='healthy', all panes in healthy list

### Scenario 2: Rate Limited Pane
Inject rate limit message, verify pane classified as rate_limited, recommendation has wait_or_caam

### Scenario 3: Unresponsive Pane
Make agent unresponsive, verify pane classified as unresponsive, fix_command is correct

### Scenario 4: --fix Auto-remediation
Create crashed pane, call --robot-diagnose --fix, verify pane recovered

### Scenario 5: Mixed Issues
Create 1 healthy, 1 rate limited, 1 crashed, verify correct classification, overall_health='degraded'

### Scenario 6: --brief Output
Verify --brief omits recommendations, summary present

### Scenario 7: --pane Filter
Create session with issues, filter to single pane, verify only that pane in output

### Scenario 8: Non-existent Pane
Call with --pane=99, verify PANE_NOT_FOUND error

## Logging Requirements
Each test logs: test name, spawn params, all ntm JSON outputs, injected conditions, timing, pass/fail

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 8 test scenarios implemented as bash scripts
2. Each scenario logs full --robot-diagnose JSON response
3. Rate limit injection reliably triggers detection
4. --fix successfully recovers crashed panes
5. Mixed issues correctly classified
6. --pane filter works correctly
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 8 test scenarios pass
2. Test logs follow JSON format specification
3. Cleanup removes all test sessions
4. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
5. Test duration under 5 minutes total
6. Each scenario logs setup/execute/verify/result
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure
8.  {"id":"bd-26y68","title":"Implement health classification engine","description":"Implement health classification engine for pane status.

## Status Categories

| Status | Detection Criteria |
|--------|-------------------|
| healthy | Agent process running, recent output (<5min), no error patterns |
| rate_limited | Output contains rate limit patterns (from bd-1mcvs) |
| unresponsive | No output for >5min AND process still running |
| crashed | No agent process OR zombie state |
| unknown | Cannot determine state |

## Implementation

Reuse status enrichments from bd-1g9wv:
- rate_limit_detected
- last_output_ts / seconds_since_output
- process_state
- child_pid

Aggregate into single classification per pane.

## Configuration

- UNRESPONSIVE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS: 300 (5 min)
- Allow override via flag or config

## Edge Cases

- New pane (no history): treat as healthy if process running
- Agent in long operation: distinguish from stuck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:22.326210325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.663555396Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.663373874Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26y68","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -3798,6 +3798,20 @@ Notes:
 - If the synthesizer mode is missing from the catalog you will see:
   `synthesis.strategy: synthesizer mode "argumentation" not found in catalog`.
 
+### Streaming synthesis output
+
+You can stream incremental synthesis chunks:
+
+```bash
+ntm ensemble synthesize mysession --stream
+ntm ensemble synthesize mysession --stream --format json  # JSONL chunks
+ntm ensemble synthesize mysession --stream --resume --run-id <id>
+```
+
+Notes:
+- `--stream --format json` emits one JSON object per line (JSONL).
+- On Ctrl+C, NTM writes a synthesis checkpoint and prints a resume command.
+
